title: persica vulgaris f. albaplena
intro: Persica vulgaris f. albaplena, also known as the white peach leaf, is a plant used in traditional herbal medicine, particularly in the Middle East and Mediterranean regions. The white peach leaf has been used to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of consuming the white peach leaf include relief from digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory problems. White peach leaf is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be dried and used in capsule or powder form as a dietary supplement.
